title: Grand Divergence
body: In the historical evolution of clinical trial registration, has the research volume gap between Africa and Europe widened across distinct regulatory epochs from 2000 to 2025? This longitudinal audit analyzed twenty-five years of interventional trial data divided into five-year epochs using the first-post-date filters from the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 database. Researchers performed an epoch-based growth analysis and reported the inter-continental volume delta as the lead estimand for global research equity trends over time. The primary result showed that while initial adoption velocity was comparable, the 2005 ICMJE mandate triggered a massive divergence where European research accelerated into a discovery orbit. Africa maintained a steady linear growth model focused on late-stage validation, failing to narrow the eight-fold volume gap established during the global expansion era. These findings reveal a structural divergence that is now deeply embedded in the global scientific infrastructure. Interpretation is limited by the retrospective registration of older trials which may obscure early adoption trends now. - DOI: 10.156/grand-divergence - Date: 2026-03-28 - Type: research - Period: 2000-2025
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
